name: Biscuits
summary: Flaky, tender sourdough-discard biscuits with buttery layers.
tips: For even flakier results, chill the shaped biscuits in the refrigerator for 15–20 minutes while you preheat the oven.
Use buttermilk instead of milk for extra flavor and tenderness.
Don't twist the cutter when shaping — it can seal the edges and prevent full rise.

steps.title: Prepare Dry Ingredients
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt.

steps.title: Cut in the Butter
steps.instruction: Add the cold cubed butter and use a pastry cutter or your fingertips to blend until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.

steps.title: Add Sourdough and Milk
steps.instruction: Add the sourdough starter and milk to the bowl. Stir just until a rough dough forms. Do not overmix.

steps.title: Fold for Layers
steps.instruction: Turn the dough onto a floured surface. Gently pat into a rectangle, then fold in thirds (like a letter). Repeat this folding 2–3 times to build flaky layers.

steps.title: Shape and Cut
steps.instruction: Pat the dough to about 1-inch thickness and cut with a biscuit cutter or floured glass. Gather scraps and re-roll as needed.
steps.tip: Don't twist the cutter — it seals the edges and limits rise.

steps.title: Chill (Optional)
steps.instruction: Chill the shaped biscuits in the fridge for 15–20 minutes while you preheat the oven for even flakier results.

steps.title: Bake
steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Place biscuits on a parchment-lined baking sheet and bake for 15–18 minutes, or until golden brown on top.

steps.title: Cool and Serve
steps.instruction: Let the biscuits cool slightly before serving. Enjoy with butter, honey, or jam!
